refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for speed; minimize teleoperation latency via low-latency network link; design safety services with non-blocking async validation.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and real-time adaptive control to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target under varying site conditions.
DP-C: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; enforce budget via design constraints and integration testing.
DP-D: Implement cycle time monitoring and telemetry reporting; use feedback to tune subsystem performance iteratively.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C (primary) + DP-D (secondary): Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems with explicit design constraints; implement continuous cycle time monitoring and telemetry reporting to enable verification and iterative tuning. This approach is traceable, measurable, and supports safety-critical validation.

potential_sources: Vehicle speed optimization may couple with safety response time (e.g., emergency stop latency).
Teleoperation latency couples with operator workload and situational awareness.
Safety service blocking can couple with cycle time if not designed asynchronously.
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP explicitly decouples via budget allocation and async design patterns. Coupling risks are manageable through interface contracts and integration testing. High safety criticality requires that safety services are never sacrificed for cycle time; budget allocation must enforce safety-first priority.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/hydraulic limits and network latency constraints.
Assumption: Site conditions (terrain, load mass, distance) remain within agreed duty cycle envelope.
Risk: If CT_target is set below physical capability, no DP can satisfy the requirement; early validation of CT_target feasibility is critical.
Risk: Network link latency variability may cause p95 to exceed target if not budgeted conservatively.
Risk: Safety service overhead (validation, interlocks) may consume budget; safety-first design may require relaxing cycle time target.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly state: 'Allocate cycle time budget with safety services as non-negotiable baseline; remaining budget distributed to vehicle and teleoperation subsystems. Budget allocation shall be validated against CT_target feasibility in Phase 1 gate review.'

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for cycle time KPI achievement. Owns the cycle time budget allocation strategy, integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems, and end-to-end cycle time measurement and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports cycle time metrics (load time, travel time, dump time, total cycle time, p95 percentile) to external monitoring/analytics systems. Enables verification and stakeholder visibility.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (load location, dump location, cycle parameters) from Site Manager or autonomous planning system. Tasking input triggers cycle execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts and emergency stop signals from Safety Services. Alerts may interrupt or abort cycle execution; cycle time measurement pauses during safety events.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation command/feedback and telemetry transmission. Network latency is a key cycle time budget component.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI. Cycle time measurement, budget allocation, and integration orchestration are core responsibilities. Minimal diffusion; all sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution hierarchy.

Vehicle owns load/travel/dump mechanical execution but does not own teleoperation latency or safety service integration. Cycle time KPI is system-level; Vehicle is a component contributor. Assigning to Vehicle would diffuse responsibility for teleoperation and safety overhead.

Teleoperation owns command latency and operator interface but does not own vehicle mechanical performance or safety service integration. Cycle time KPI spans all subsystems; assigning to Teleoperation would obscure vehicle and safety contributions.

Safety Services owns safety validation and interlocks but is not responsible for cycle time optimization. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would create conflict of interest (safety vs. speed) and diffuse responsibility for vehicle and teleoperation performance.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance metric that integrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems. (2) NGHE Solution owns the budget allocation strategy and integration orchestration. (3) Cycle time measurement and reporting (via Telemetry interface) is a system-level responsibility. (4) Assigning to any sub-component would diffuse responsibility and create coupling conflicts.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services are designed independently without cycle time budget constraints, NGHE Solution may lack authority to enforce budget compliance.
Risk: Cycle time measurement may require instrumentation across multiple subsystems; NGHE Solution must define measurement interfaces and data collection protocols.
Risk: If site conditions or duty cycle change post-deployment, NGHE Solution must have authority to re-allocate budget or adjust CT_target; governance model must be clear.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit cycle time budget allocation document owned by NGHE Solution, with sign-off from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services leads. Define measurement protocol and telemetry schema for cycle time reporting. Establish change control process for CT_target adjustments.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry monitoring) directly addresses FR (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target). DP is traceable, measurable, and supports safety-critical validation. Coupling risks are identified and manageable. Assumptions about CT_target feasibility and site conditions are explicit and can be validated in Phase 1.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution lacks authority over Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services budget allocation, DP cannot be enforced. Mitigation: Establish governance model and budget allocation document with cross-functional sign-off.

Risk: Cycle time measurement may require instrumentation in Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems; NGHE Solution must define measurement interfaces. Mitigation: Define telemetry schema and measurement protocol in Phase 1.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for cycle time KPI ownership. Budget allocation, measurement, and reporting are system-level responsibilities. Sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution hierarchy and must comply with budget constraints.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (Telemetry interface). Verification approach: instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 computation from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target. Feasibility is high if CT_target is validated as achievable in Phase 1.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time budget allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns overall KPI and budget strategy; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own sub-budgets. Allocation must be documented and enforced via design constraints and integration testing.

recommended_revisions: Validate CT_target feasibility against vehicle kinematic/hydraulic limits, network latency, and safety service overhead in Phase 1. If CT_target is infeasible, escalate to stakeholders for negotiation.
Define cycle time budget allocation document with explicit sub-budgets for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Obtain cross-functional sign-off.
Define telemetry schema and measurement protocol for cycle time reporting. Specify data collection points (load start, travel start, dump start, cycle end) and p95 computation method.
Establish change control process for CT_target adjustments post-deployment. Define governance model for budget re-allocation if site conditions change.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing vehicle performance, teleoperation latency, safety service overhead, and measurement/reporting. Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry) is sound and traceable, but implementation requires detailed design of sub-components. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM owner, but sub-FRs must be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services for detailed design. Proceed to next decomposition level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated cycle time budget (e.g., load ≤ T_load, travel ≤ T_travel, dump ≤ T_dump).
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce command latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget (e.g., latency ≤ T_latency).
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall validate safety conditions and enforce interlocks without blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure cycle time metrics (load time, travel time, dump time, total cycle time) and report p95 percentile via Telemetry interface.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/hydraulic limits and network latency constraints.
Site conditions (terrain, load mass, distance) remain within agreed duty cycle envelope.
Safety services can be designed with non-blocking async validation to avoid cycle time impact.
Telemetry interface supports continuous cycle time measurement and p95 reporting.
NGHE Solution has authority to enforce cycle time budget allocation across sub-components.
open_risks: Risk: CT_target may be infeasible if set below physical capability; early validation required.
Risk: Network link latency variability may cause p95 to exceed target; conservative budgeting required.
Risk: Safety service overhead may consume budget; safety-first design may require relaxing cycle time target.
Risk: Site conditions may change post-deployment; governance model for budget re-allocation must be established.
Risk: Cycle time measurement may require instrumentation across multiple subsystems; measurement protocol must be defined.
evidence_needed: Phase 1 validation: CT_target feasibility analysis against vehicle kinematic/hydraulic limits, network latency, and safety service overhead.
Cycle time budget allocation document with explicit sub-budgets for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
Telemetry schema and measurement protocol for cycle time reporting.
Integration test plan for cycle time KPI verification under representative duty cycle.
Safety analysis: confirmation that safety services can be designed with non-blocking async validation.
